LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WACHOVIA BANK, N.A. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT PRODUCED NO EVIDENCE ALLOWING AN INFERENCE THAT PLAINTIFF NEITHER POSSESSES, NOR REASONABLY CAN OBTAIN, EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT ITS CAUSES OF ACTION. FURTHERMORE, A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT IS RAISED WHETHER PROPER NOTICE WAS GIVEN WITH RESPECT TO THE TERMINATION DATE. EXHIBIT G TO THE MAHER DECLARATION PROVIDES A PHONE NUMBER, NOT A FAX NUMBER. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER INSTRUCTIONS WERE PROVIDED FROM ESC TO FAX CORRESPONDENCE TO MS. LUCHESE AND/OR TO FAX AT ANY PARTICULAR NUMBER. NO EVIDENCE THAT ESC IN ANY WAY INTERFERED WITH DEFENDANT'S NOTICE ATTEMPTS. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER DEFENDANT COMPLIED WITH NOTICE REQUIREMENT WHEN TIMELY TELEPHONIC NOTICE NOT PROVIDED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WACHOVIA BANK, N.A., Motion for summary judgment, or, in the alternative, summary adjudication. Appearances: Adam Hofmann, Esq. & James Napoli, Esq. of Hanson Bridgett (415) 777-3200, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Heather B. Hoesterey, Esq. of ReedSmith LLP (415) 659-5958 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment, or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied, failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendant produced no evidence allowing an inference that plaintiff neither possesses, nor reasonably can obtain, evidence to support its causes of action. Furthermore, a triable issue of material fact is raised whether proper notice was given with respect to the termination date. Exhibit G to the Maher declaration provides a phone number, not a fax number. Triable issue of fact whether instructions were provided from ESC to fax correspondence to Ms. Luchese and/or to fax at any particular number. No evidence that ESC in any way interfered with defendant's notice attempts. Triable issue of fact whether defendant complied with notice requirement when timely telephonic notice not provided. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
